title: IDF Strikes Kill At Least 5 Palestinians in Gaza, Including 9-Year-Old Boy in Khan Younis
detail: Israeli strikes on April 28, 2026 killed at least five Palestinians in Gaza, including a 9-year-old boy named Adel al-Najjar who was collecting firewood in eastern Khan Younis. Hospital authorities at Nasser Medical Complex confirmed the child's death from Israeli fire. A separate drone strike hit a vehicle in Gaza City, killing two Palestinians. The strikes continue a pattern of daily Israeli military action inside Gaza despite the October 2025 ceasefire framework. Since the ceasefire began on October 10, 2025, the OIC Media Observatory has documented 824 Palestinians killed (including 200+ children) and 2,318 wounded by Israeli forces — a toll confirmed in an April 28 OIC statement. Israel has conducted strikes on 36 of the past 40 days as of early April 2026.

media.caption: At least five Palestinians killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza, including 9-year-old Adel al-Najjar in Khan Younis

title: UN Security Council: Gaza Ceasefire 'Increasingly Fragile' as Toll Since Truce Reaches 824 Killed
detail: UN Assistant Secretary-General Khaled Khiari formally briefed the UN Security Council on April 28, 2026 that the Gaza ceasefire is 'increasingly fragile.' Khiari stated that approximately 800 Palestinians (including 200+ children) and 7 humanitarian workers have been killed since the October 10, 2025 ceasefire began, with 1.8 million people remaining displaced and dependent on aid. He noted that Phase 2 negotiations on Hamas disarmament and a permanent political solution have produced no agreement, raising the risk of a return to full hostilities. The UN formal statement came on the same day the OIC Palestine Observatory updated its count to 824 killed and 2,318 wounded by Israeli forces since the ceasefire. The ASG's remarks followed the UNSC open debate on the Palestinian question held earlier in the week, during which members pressed for Phase 2 implementation and unimpeded humanitarian access.

media.caption: UN Security Council briefed: Gaza ceasefire 'increasingly fragile' with 824 Palestinians killed since October 2025 truce
